messages.content: How to add item in inventory in quickbook online

To add an item in inventory in QuickBooks Online, you can follow these steps:

1. Sign in to your QuickBooks Online account.
2. Click on the "Sales" tab in the left navigation menu.
3. Select "Products and Services".
4. Click on the "New" button at the top right corner of the Products and Services page.
5. Choose the type of item you want to add (Inventory, Non-inventory, Service, or Bundle).
6. Provide the necessary details for the item, including the name, description, SKU, sales price, purchase cost, and quantity on hand.
7. If you selected "Inventory" type, make sure to enable the "I track quantity on hand" option and enter the starting quantity and the starting date.
8. If needed, you can also add an image for the item.
9. Once you have entered all the required information, click on the "Save and close" button to add the item to your inventory.

Note: It is important to keep accurate inventory records, so make sure to update the quantity on hand whenever you make a sale or receive new inventory.
Creating customer estimate in Quick book on line

To create a customer estimate in QuickBooks Online, you can follow these steps:

1. Sign in to your QuickBooks Online account.
2. Click on the "Sales" tab in the navigation menu on the left.
3. Select "Estimates" from the dropdown menu.
4. Click on the "New Estimate" button at the top right corner of the Estimates page.
5. Fill out the customer information by selecting an existing customer from the drop-down menu or click on "+ Add new" to create a new customer.
6. Enter the estimate details, such as the estimate date, expiration date, and the terms and conditions.
7. Add the products/services you are estimating by selecting an existing item from the drop-down menu or click on "+ Add new" to create a new item.
8. Specify the quantity, rate, and any additional information for each item.
9. If needed, you can add more lines for additional items or expenses by clicking on "+ Add lines" at the bottom.
10. Include any necessary notes or messages to the customer in the "Message displayed on estimate" section.
11. Review the estimate to ensure accuracy.
12. Once everything is complete, click on the "Save and send" button to save the estimate and send it to the customer.
13. You can choose to send the estimate via email, share a link with the customer, or print it.
14. QuickBooks Online will automatically track the status of the estimate as either "Pending," "Accepted," "Closed," or "Rejected" as the customer responds.
how to enter customer deposit in quick book online

To enter a customer deposit in QuickBooks Online, you can follow these steps:

1. Sign in to your QuickBooks Online account.
2. Click on the "+" icon at the top right corner of the screen.
3. Under the "Customers" section, select "Receive Payment".
4. In the "Receive Payment" window, select the customer for whom you want to record the deposit. If the customer is not in your list, click on "+ Add new" to create a new customer.
5. In the "Outstanding Transactions" section, select the invoice(s) for which you are receiving the deposit. If there is a specific invoice, you can enter the amount under the "Amount received" column. If you are receiving a deposit without any specific invoice, leave the "Amount received" field empty.
6. In the "Deposit to" section, select the bank account where you want to deposit the customer's payment.
7. In the "Payment method" section, choose the payment method for the customer's deposit (e.g., cash, check, credit card, etc.).
8. If needed, you can enter a reference number and a memo to provide additional information about the deposit.
9. Review the payment details to ensure accuracy.
10. Once everything is complete, click on the "Save and close" button to record the customer deposit in QuickBooks Online.

Now, the customer deposit will be recorded and reflected in your customer's account as a credit until it is applied to an invoice or refunded.
how to record customer down payment without invoice in quickbook online

To record a customer down payment without an invoice in QuickBooks Online, you can follow these steps:

1. Sign in to your QuickBooks Online account.
2. Click on the "+" icon at the top right corner of the screen.
3. Under the "Customers" section, select "Receive Payment".
4. In the "Receive Payment" window, select the customer for whom you want to record the down payment. If the customer is not in your list, click on "+ Add new" to create a new customer.
5. In the "Outstanding Transactions" section, leave it blank as there is no specific invoice for this down payment.
6. In the "Deposit to" section, select the bank account where you want to deposit the customer's payment.
7. Enter the amount of the down payment in the "Payment amount" field.
8. In the "Payment method" section, choose the payment method for the customer's down payment (e.g., cash, check, credit card, etc.).
9. If needed, you can enter a reference number and a memo to provide additional information about the down payment.
10. Review the payment details to ensure accuracy.
11. Once everything is complete, click on the "Save and close" button to record the customer down payment in QuickBooks Online.

The down payment will now be recorded and reflected as a credit in the customer's account until it is applied to an invoice or refunded.
how to enter customer deposit as current liability instead of credit in receivable in quick book online

To enter a customer deposit as a current liability instead of a credit in receivables in QuickBooks Online, you can follow these steps:

1. Sign in to your QuickBooks Online account.
2. Click on the "+" icon at the top right corner of the screen.
3. Under the "Customers" section, select "Receive Payment".
4. In the "Receive Payment" window, select the customer for whom you want to record the deposit. If the customer is not in your list, click on "+ Add new" to create a new customer.
5. In the "Outstanding Transactions" section, leave it blank as there is no specific invoice for this deposit.
6. In the "Deposit to" section, select the bank account where you want to deposit the customer's payment.
7. Enter the amount of the deposit in the "Payment amount" field.
8. In the "Payment method" section, choose the payment method for the customer's deposit (e.g., cash, check, credit card, etc.).
9. If needed, you can enter a reference number and a memo to provide additional information about the deposit.
10. In the "Category" section, click on the "Add new deposit" link.
11. In the "Account" field, select "Other Current Liabilities" or create a new liability account called "Customer Deposits" if it doesn't exist.
12. Enter the amount of the deposit in the "Amount" field.
13. Click on the "Save and close" button to record the customer deposit as a current liability in QuickBooks Online.

Now, the customer deposit will be recorded as a current liability on your balance sheet, reflecting the funds you have received from the customer.
how to enter customer deposit as current liability instead of credit in receivable in quick book online and where can I find category section

I apologize for the confusion. In QuickBooks Online, the category section does not appear in the "Receive Payment" window when recording customer deposits. As a result, you cannot directly assign the customer deposit as a current liability during the payment entry. However, you can manually create a liability account for customer deposits and use the "Journal Entry" feature to record the deposit as a current liability. Here's how:

1. Sign in to your QuickBooks Online account.
2. Click on the "+" icon at the top right corner of the screen.
3. Under the "Other" section, select "Journal Entry".
4. In the "Journal Entry" window, select the date of the deposit.
5. In the "Account" section, select the liability account where you want to record the customer deposit (e.g., "Customer Deposits").
6. Enter the deposit amount in the "Debits" column.
7. In the "Name" or "Account" column, select the customer from whom you received the deposit.
8. In the "Name" or "Account" column, choose the bank account where you deposited the customer payment.
9. Click on the "+ Add another line" link to add new lines if you have multiple deposits from different customers.
10. Review the details to ensure accuracy.
11. Once everything is complete, click on the "Save and close" button to record the journal entry and record the customer deposit as a liability.

Please note that this process requires a bit more manual entry, but it allows you to designate the customer deposit as a current liability.

how to enter customer deposit as current liability instead of credit in receivable in quick book online and how can I enter the customer name as a sub-account under current liability account "Customer deposit" which I already set up

To enter a customer deposit as a current liability and assign the customer name as a sub-account under the current liability account "Customer Deposit" in QuickBooks Online, you can follow these steps:

1. Sign in to your QuickBooks Online account.
2. Click on the "Accounting" tab in the left navigation menu.
3. Select "Chart of Accounts".
4. Locate the "Customer Deposit" current liability account from the list of accounts and click on it to edit.
5. In the "Account Name" field, edit the name to include the customer's name, for example, "Customer Deposit - [Customer Name]".
6. Click on the "Save and Close" button to save the change.

Now, you have created a sub-account for the specific customer under the "Customer Deposit" current liability account. 

To record the customer deposit and assign it to the sub-account:
1. Click on the "+" icon at the top right corner of the screen.
2. Under the "Customers" section, select "Receive Payment".
3. In the "Receive Payment" window, select the customer for whom you want to record the deposit.
4. In the "Outstanding Transactions" section, leave it blank as there is no specific invoice for this deposit.
5. In the "Deposit to" section, select the bank account where you want to deposit the customer's payment.
6. Enter the amount of the deposit in the "Payment amount" field.
7. In the "Payment method" section, choose the payment method for the customer's deposit (e.g., cash, check, credit card, etc.).
8. If needed, you can enter a reference number and a memo to provide additional information about the deposit.
9. In the "Category" section, select the sub-account you created under the "Customer Deposit" current liability account. The sub-account will be listed under the "Liabilities and Credit Card" section.
10. Click on the "Save and close" button to record the customer deposit as a current liability with the assigned sub-account.

Now, you have successfully recorded the customer deposit as a current liability and assigned it to the sub-account under "Customer Deposit" in QuickBooks Online.
how to add subaccount under customer deposit

To add a sub-account under the "Customer Deposit" account in QuickBooks Online, you can follow these steps:

1. Sign in to your QuickBooks Online account.
2. Click on the "Accounting" tab in the left navigation menu.
3. Select "Chart of Accounts".
4. Locate the "Customer Deposit" account from the list of accounts and click on it to edit.
5. Click on the "New" button in the upper-right corner of the screen.
6. In the "Account Type" drop-down menu, select "Other Current Liability" or the appropriate account type for your sub-account.
7. In the "Detail Type" drop-down menu, choose the most appropriate option for your sub-account.
8. In the "Name" field, enter the name for the sub-account, such as the specific customer's name.
9. If desired, provide a description and add any other necessary details.
10. Click on the "Save and Close" button to save the sub-account.

Now, you have successfully added a sub-account under the "Customer Deposit" account. This sub-account can be used to record customer deposits specifically for that particular customer.
how to delete a journal entry in quick book on line

To delete a journal entry in QuickBooks Online, you can follow these steps:

1. Sign in to your QuickBooks Online account.
2. Click on the "Accounting" tab in the left navigation menu.
3. Select "Chart of Accounts".
4. Locate the appropriate account associated with the journal entry you want to delete and click on it to view the account register.
5. Locate the journal entry you want to delete within the account register.
6. Select the journal entry by clicking on the checkbox next to it. You can select multiple entries if needed.
7. Once the journal entry is selected, click on the "Batch actions" drop-down menu above the account register.
8. From the drop-down menu, choose "Delete".
9. A confirmation message will appear asking if you are sure you want to delete the journal entry. Click on "Yes" to confirm the deletion.

Please note that once deleted, the journal entry cannot be restored. Therefore, double-check to ensure that you are deleting the correct journal entry before confirming the deletion.
how to generate inventory report that contains volume and value on quickbook online

To generate an inventory report that contains volume and value in QuickBooks Online, you can follow these steps:

1. Sign in to your QuickBooks Online account.
2. Click on the "Reports" tab in the left navigation menu.
3. In the search bar at the top, type "Inventory Valuation Summary" and press Enter.
4. Select the "Inventory Valuation Summary" report from the search results.
5. Set the appropriate date range for the report using the "Report period" field.
6. Click on "Customize" at the top-right corner of the report.
7. In the "Customize" window, go to the "Rows/Columns" tab.
8. Under the "Columns" section, click on "Change columns".
9. Scroll down and select "Quantity on Hand" to include the volume information in the report.
10. Scroll further down and ensure that "Inventory Value" is selected to include the value information in the report.
11. Click on "Run report" to generate the inventory report with volume and value.

The generated report will provide information about the quantity on hand (volume) and the value of each item in your inventory, helping you analyze your inventory levels and their respective worth.
how to enter customer payment using customer deposit recorded as current liability

To enter a customer payment using a previously recorded customer deposit as a current liability in QuickBooks Online, you can follow these steps:

1. Sign in to your QuickBooks Online account.
2. Click on the "+" icon at the top right corner of the screen.
3. Under the "Customers" section, select "Receive Payment".
4. In the "Receive Payment" window, select the customer for whom you want to record the payment.
5. In the "Outstanding Transactions" section, locate the invoice that the customer payment is applied to and select it.
6. Enter the payment amount in the "Payment" field.
7. In the "Deposit to" section, select the bank account where you want to deposit the customer's payment.
8. In the "Payment method" section, choose the appropriate payment method (e.g., cash, check, credit card, etc.).
9. If needed, you can enter a reference number and a memo to provide additional information about the payment.
10. Scroll down to the "Credits" section and click on "Apply credit".
11. In the "Available credits" section, select the appropriate customer deposit from the list. This will offset the payment amount with the customer deposit.
12. If the payment exceeds the amount of the customer deposit, enter any remaining overpayment in the "Payment" field.
13. Review the payment details to ensure accuracy.
14. Click on the "Save and close" button to record the customer payment, applying the customer deposit as a current liability.

By following these steps, you can record a customer payment using a previously recorded customer deposit as a current liability in QuickBooks Online, ensuring accurate tracking of customer balances and liabilities.
how to enter customer payment using customer deposit recorded as current liability, when customer deposit is not entered as credit, but as liability under customer deposit

If the customer deposit was recorded as a liability under a separate account named "Customer Deposit" instead of being entered as a credit in accounts receivable, you can follow these steps to enter a customer payment using that customer deposit in QuickBooks Online:

1. Sign in to your QuickBooks Online account.
2. Click on the "+" icon at the top right corner of the screen.
3. Under the "Customers" section, select "Receive Payment".
4. In the "Receive Payment" window, select the customer for whom you want to record the payment.
5. In the "Outstanding Transactions" section, select the invoice(s) that the customer payment is applied to, if applicable.
6. Enter the payment amount in the "Payment" field.
7. In the "Deposit to" section, select the bank account where you want to deposit the customer's payment.
8. In the "Payment method" section, choose the appropriate payment method (e.g., cash, check, credit card, etc.).
9. If needed, enter a reference number and a memo to provide additional information about the payment.
10. In the "Credits" section, click on "Add credits".
11. From the "Apply credits" window, select the liability account you recorded the customer deposit under (e.g., "Customer Deposit").
12. Enter the amount of the customer deposit in the "Amount" column.
13. Click on the "Done" button to apply the customer deposit as a payment credit.
14. Review the payment details to ensure accuracy.
15. Click on the "Save and close" button to record the customer payment using the customer deposit as a current liability.

In this method, you are applying the customer deposit from the liability account against the payment, effectively reducing the amount owed by the customer. QuickBooks Online will automatically adjust the customer balance and reflect the payment transaction accordingly.
I can not find credit section in receiving payments. Where can I find it?

I apologize for the confusion. In QuickBooks Online, there is no specific "Credit" section in the "Receive Payment" window to directly apply customer deposits recorded as liabilities. Instead, you can follow an alternative approach to apply the customer deposit towards the payment. Here's how:

1. Sign in to your QuickBooks Online account.
2. Click on the "+" icon at the top right corner of the screen.
3. Under the "Customers" section
